#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Afghanistan-Pakistan War Update — Apr 01, 2026

Pakistan formally announced the full resumption of Operation Ghazab-il-Haq on March 31, declaring the campaign would continue 'until its objectives are achieved.' On March 30, the Pakistani Air Force conducted its widest single-day strike package of the conflict, hitting 41 locations including Taliban brigade and battalion headquarters in Nangarhar and an army HQ in Kandahar, killing 1 and wounding 16 civilians according to Afghan officials. UN Human Rights Chief Türk issued a formal condemnation calling the conflict a situation that 'piles misery on misery' and pleading for dialogue. The Torkham border crossing partially reopened on March 31 for Afghan national returns only, while trade and passenger services remained suspended. By April 1, the conflict had entered its fifth week with no ceasefire framework in sight, drawing CFR analysis describing it as a 'low-intensity interstate war' unlikely to resolve through negotiation in the near term.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, mapPoints, kpis, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/afghanistan-pakistan-war/

China Tech Revolution Update — Apr 01, 2026

Huawei launched the Ascend 950PR AI inference chip with ByteDance and Alibaba placing immediate orders for ~750,000 units planned through 2026, marking a major step in China's CUDA-compatible domestic AI compute transition. SMIC reported record 2025 revenue of $9.33 billion (+20.9% YoY) with a 2026 action plan targeting above-industry growth — even as unverified US allegations of chip equipment transfers to Iran persisted; China's Foreign Ministry dismissed the claims as 'false information'. DeepSeek suffered its longest outage since launch (13+ hours, March 29-30), exposing infrastructure reliability gaps, while Zhipu AI surged 35% in Hong Kong and ByteDance reportedly plans $14 billion in Nvidia GPU purchases for 2026. China's AI industry crossed 1.2 trillion yuan in core value with 6,200+ enterprises, and the Xiong'an AI Forum unveiled incentives for AI integration into traditional manufacturing sectors.

Sections updated: timeline, mapPoints, kpis, econ, claims

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/china-tech-revolution/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Gaza War Update — Apr 01, 2026

Houthis launched their third consecutive ballistic missile attack on Israel on April 1 — Passover — claiming joint operations with Iran and Hezbollah; all were intercepted with no casualties. Hezbollah also struck northern Israel on March 31 with a rocket barrage, causing three light injuries. UN OCHA warned that Gaza faces renewed famine conditions by mid-April if all land crossings remain shut, as the 2026 Flash Appeal stays 93% unfunded. The cumulative Gaza death toll stands at 72,285 confirmed killed, with 680+ killed since the October 2025 ceasefire; all 37 banned humanitarian organizations remain suspended from operations.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, kpis, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/gaza-war/

Global Recession Risk Update — Apr 1, 2026

JPMorgan raised its US recession probability to 60% — the highest of any major investment bank — as Liberation Day approached its one-year anniversary with $166 billion in IEEPA tariff revenue under legal threat and the tariff regime widely assessed as having failed its own metrics. China's NBS Manufacturing PMI surged back to expansion at 50.4 in March (first expansion since March 2025), though input prices hit a 4-year high from Iran-conflict commodity costs. President Trump opened his first China visit since 2017 on April 1 in Beijing, with the Trump-Xi summit focusing on agriculture purchases, rare earth concessions, and fentanyl enforcement amid China's record $213.6 billion trade surplus. Markets rallied on Iran ceasefire signals (S&P +0.7%, Brent crude falling to $98.79/barrel, 10Y yield retreating to ~4.30%), while Conference Board Consumer Confidence Expectations remained at 70.9 — the 14th consecutive month below the 80-point recession-warning threshold. Core PCE re-accelerated to 3.06% year-over-year in January 2026 BEA data (released March 13).

Sections updated: kpis, econ, timeline, mapPoints, claims, political, events

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/global-recession-risk/

https://watchboard.dev/haiti-collapse/

#DisasterTracking #OSINT #Watchboard

Haiti Collapse Update — Apr 01, 2026

Gran Grif gang launched a pre-dawn massacre on March 30 in Jean-Denis, Artibonite, killing at least 16 confirmed and up to 70 per rights groups (RNDDH), wounding ~30 and displacing nearly 6,000 — the second major Gran Grif atrocity in Artibonite following October 2024's Pont-Sondé massacre. The Jean-Denis attack site was added to the map and a new casualty entry recorded with evolving figures. The MSS effectiveness claim was updated to reflect PassBlue's March 30 analysis questioning whether the Gang Suppression Force will meet its 5,550-person target, given US funding complications and Chad's uncertain participation. Meta headline and daycount updated to reflect the massacre and current crisis state.

Sections updated: events, mapPoints, casualties, kpis, claims, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/haiti-collapse/

#OSINT #Security #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

ICE History Update — Apr 01, 2026

Markwayne Mullin replaced Kristi Noem as DHS Secretary as the DHS partial shutdown reached Day 46 with Congress on two-week recess. Jose Guadalupe Ramos-Solano died at Adelanto ICE Processing Center on March 25 — the 14th ICE custody death of 2026 and the 47th since January 2025; ICE has stopped paying for third-party medical care since October 2025. On March 31, the U.S. flew 17 migrants to El Salvador's CECOT prison despite a federal court TRO, prompting a DOJ whistleblower to allege knowing violation of the court order. A Mass Deportation Coalition called for 'Phase II' enforcement targeting worksites and financial records. Judge Dolly Gee barred ICE from re-detaining a cognitively disabled man who had been deported without understanding the process.

Sections updated: events, kpis, mapPoints, casualties, claims, political, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/ice-history/

Mencho/CJNG Update — Apr 01, 2026

Day 38 of the post-Mencho succession crisis: O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) maintains control under a collective council structure enforced by Grupo Élite's paramilitary capacity, with no new senior-command-level captures confirmed. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) is now being named in narcocorridos as El Mencho's successor — a culturally significant intelligence signal reflecting intensifying internal CJNG power competition. Operativo Enjambre has cumulatively produced 64+ official arrests across Jalisco, Michoacán, and additional states. A new Puerto Vallarta CJNG timeshare fraud network map point documents the February 2026 OFAC sanctions of Kovay Gardens and associated entities, reflecting continued U.S.-Mexico bilateral financial intelligence coordination.

Sections updated: events, kpis, meta, mapPoints, political

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/mencho-cjng/

https://watchboard.dev/myanmar-civil-war/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 01, 2026

Min Aung Hlaing was elected vice-president on April 1 by Myanmar's junta-packed lower house with 247 of 260 votes, completing the formal step before a predetermined presidential elevation. He had retired as military Commander-in-Chief on March 30 — installing loyalist General Ye Win Oo — to meet the civilian candidacy requirement under the 2008 constitution. The Arakan Army maintained its Sittwe siege through March 31 with approximately 48 junta soldiers deserting as fighters held positions 2 km from the city center. On March 30, SAC jets killed approximately 10 civilians including pregnant women in Butalin Township, Sagaing Region in a 75-minute airstrike — continuing the documented pattern of deliberate civilian targeting. The days-since-coup counter reached 1,885.

Sections updated: events, timeline, mapPoints, mapLines, kpis, political, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/myanmar-civil-war/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

NATO-US Tensions Update — Apr 1, 2026

Defense Secretary Hegseth refused to reaffirm US commitment to Article 5 on March 31, telling reporters it was 'the president's decision' — the first time a US SecDef has conditioned the mutual defense clause on presidential discretion. Trump escalated further on April 1, calling NATO a 'paper tiger' and saying he is 'strongly considering' withdrawing the US from the alliance. Secretary of State Rubio simultaneously pledged to 're-examine the value of NATO' after the Iran war concludes. European allies responded with a coordinated pushback: Spain closed its airspace to US jets, Italy denied landing rights at Sigonella, and Poland refused to redeploy Patriot batteries — while France, Germany, and Poland issued a Weimar Triangle joint statement reaffirming European security commitments. UK PM Starmer announced Britain would host a ~35-nation diplomatic Hormuz meeting and defended NATO as 'the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen.'

Sections updated: events, kpis, mapPoints, econ, claims, political

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/nato-us-tensions/

Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Apr 1, 2026

President Sheinbaum launched 'Plan Michoacán for Peace and Justice' — a 57 billion peso, 12-point security and development strategy — following protests in Uruapan and Morelia demanding action against persistent CJNG violence. She defended Mexico's right to supply oil to Cuba despite Trump's tariff threat, confirming Pemex is working to restore crude deliveries cut to ~7,000 bpd. The Buenavista–AIFA suburban train missed its Holy Week inauguration target again, delayed indefinitely pending safety certification ahead of the FIFA World Cup. Sheinbaum unveiled 18 new programs under an expanded Plan México at the National Museum of Anthropology. The U.S. Section 301 tariff probe comment period opened with a Mexican government response due April 15.

Sections updated: events, mapPoints, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/sheinbaum-presidency/

Sinaloa Fragmentation Update — Apr 01, 2026

El Mayo Zambada's April 13 sentencing before Judge Brian Cogan (EDNY) is now 12 days away; the US government's response to his defense sentencing memo is due April 6, ahead of what will be the formal legal closure of the Sinaloa Cartel co-founder's 50-year career — mandatory life imprisonment plus a $15 billion forfeiture judgment. The civil war has now reached 569 days since the September 9, 2024 outbreak, with 2,400+ homicides and 9,000+ families displaced. Semana Santa security operations were launched across Sinaloa on March 29 with joint SEDENA/SEMAR/state police deployments. No new major armed clashes or territorial changes were confirmed in the March 25–31 gap window; La Mayiza's ~90% territorial advantage remains stable.

Sections updated: kpis, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/sinaloa-fragmentation/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Somalia Conflict Update — Apr 01, 2026

Somalia's federal crisis reached its most acute phase when SNA forces seized Baidoa (Southwest State capital) on March 30, forcing President Laftagareen's resignation — the first federal military takeover of a member state capital in Somalia's history. Puntland withdrew recognition of the Federal Government on March 31, leaving Somalia's governance architecture severely fractured as parliamentary mandates approach expiry (April 14). On the military front, SNA and AUSSOM repelled an Al-Shabaab assault at Mubarak (40 killed, March 26), killed 21 fighters in Bay Region's Daynuunay (March 29), and AFRICOM conducted a confirmed strike 132 km northwest of Kismayo (March 27). UNHCR confirmed 50,000+ newly displaced by Southwest State clashes, while UNICEF warned that 6.5 million Somalis face acute hunger with the humanitarian funding gap widening to ~76% of the $852M requirement.

Sections updated: events, mapPoints, mapLines, kpis, casualties, econ, claims, political, assets, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/somalia-conflict/

Southeast Asia Escalation Update — Apr 01, 2026

AFP Chief Gen. Brawner confirmed Balikatan 2026 starts ~April 20, with Japan sending combat-capable JSDF units carrying weapons for the first time since World War II — the largest iteration of the exercise in history, with Australia and France also participating. Simultaneously, three Chinese coast guard cutters were tracked bracketing the Philippines' northernmost Batanes Islands near the Bashi Channel in what analyst Ray Powell called 'a new level of aggression.' On March 30, China published a combat readiness patrol video near Scarborough Shoal featuring Type 054A frigates and J-15 carrier-based jets. Marcos EO 111, signed March 26 and made public March 31, officially mandates Philippine names for 131 Kalayaan Island Group features in all government agencies and school curricula. AFP's March 2026 monthly tally recorded 90 unauthorized Chinese vessels in the West Philippine Sea.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, kpis, political, claims, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/southeast-asia-escalation/

https://watchboard.dev/spacex-history/

#Space #OSINT #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

SpaceX History Update — Apr 01, 2026

SpaceX closed March 2026 with a historic double-launch day on March 30: Transporter-16 orbited 119 payloads from Vandenberg (booster B1093, 12th flight), crossing the 1,600-total-rideshare-payload milestone, while Starlink 10-44 from Cape Canaveral saw booster B1067 set a world record with its 34th flight — the most flights ever by a single orbital rocket stage. The two March 30 launches pushed SpaceX's 2026 total to 40 missions and all-time booster recoveries to 593. Starship IFT-12 preparations advanced with Ship 39 completing cryoloading preflight objectives at Starbase; the FAA license window runs April 5–October 5, 2026, with a late-April target pending a full Booster 19 static fire. The SpaceX IPO process is advancing, with investor meetings scheduled for April and Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs confirmed as lead underwriters.

Sections updated: kpis, claims, political, events

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/spacex-history/
